examples.title: CSMA/CD Protocol Simulation
examples.description: Simulates basic CSMA/CD behavior with carrier sense and collision detection
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CSMA_CD
BEGIN
  MAX_ATTEMPTS ← 16
  SLOT_TIME ← 512 bits (51.2μs for 10Mbps Ethernet)
  
  FUNCTION transmit(frame)
    attempts ← 0
    WHILE attempts < MAX_ATTEMPTS DO
      WHILE channel_busy() DO
        wait_idle()
      END WHILE
      
      start_transmission(frame)
      WHILE transmitting DO
        IF collision_detected() THEN
          stop_transmission()
          send_jam_signal()
          wait_time ← random(0, 2^attempts - 1) * SLOT_TIME
          wait(wait_time)
          attempts ← attempts + 1
          BREAK
        END IF
      END WHILE
      
      IF NOT collision_detected() THEN
        RETURN SUCCESS
      END IF
    END WHILE
    
    RETURN FAILURE
  END FUNCTION
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the CSMA/CD algorithm: listen before talking, detect collisions while transmitting, send jam signal on collision, and use binary exponential backoff before retrying.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) per successful transmission, O(2^n) worst case for collisions

examples.title: Ethernet Frame Parser
examples.description: Parses and displays Ethernet frame fields from raw bytes
examples.code:
def parse_ethernet_frame(raw_bytes):
    '''Parse Ethernet frame and return dictionary of fields'''
    if len(raw_bytes) < 14:
        raise ValueError("Frame too short - minimum 14 bytes header")
    
    # Extract fields from raw bytes
    dest_mac = raw_bytes[0:6]
    src_mac = raw_bytes[6:12]
    ether_type = raw_bytes[12:14]
    payload = raw_bytes[14:]
    
    # Format MAC addresses as hex: XX:XX:XX:XX:XX:XX
    dest_mac_str = ':'.join(f'{b:02x}' for b in dest_mac)
    src_mac_str = ':'.join(f'{b:02x}' for b in src_mac)
    
    # Parse EtherType (big-endian)
    ether_type_int = (ether_type[0] << 8) | ether_type[1]
    ether_type_map = {
        0x0800: 'IPv4',
        0x0806: 'ARP',
        0x86DD: 'IPv6',
        0x8100: 'VLAN Tag'
    }
    protocol = ether_type_map.get(ether_type_int, f'Unknown (0x{ether_type_int:04x})')
    
    return {
        'destination_mac': dest_mac_str,
        'source_mac': src_mac_str,
        'ether_type': ether_type_int,
        'protocol': protocol,
        'payload_length': len(payload),
        'total_frame_size': len(raw_bytes)
    }

# Example usage
frame_bytes = bytes([
    0xff, 0xff, 0xff, 0xff, 0xff, 0xff,  # Broadcast dest
    0x00, 0x1a, 0x2b, 0x3c, 0x4d, 0x5e,  # Source MAC
    0x08, 0x00,                          # IPv4 EtherType
    # IPv4 packet would follow here...
])

result = parse_ethernet_frame(frame_bytes)
for key, value in result.items():
    print(f"{key}: {value}")
examples.explanation: This Python code parses raw Ethernet frame bytes, extracting destination/source MAC addresses, EtherType field (identifying the upper-layer protocol), and displaying the frame structure.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is frame size

examples.title: Binary Exponential Backoff Algorithm
examples.description: Implements the backoff algorithm used after collision detection in Ethernet
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Binary_Exponential_Backoff
BEGIN
  MAX_BACKOFF ← 10        // After 10 collisions, use max 1023 slots
  MAX_ATTEMPTS ← 16       // Give up after 16 attempts
  SLOT_TIME ← 512 bits   // 51.2 microseconds for 10Mbps
  
  FUNCTION calculate_backoff(collision_count)
    // k = min(collision_count, 10) - limit exponent to 10
    k ← MIN(collision_count, MAX_BACKOFF)
    
    // Generate random number from 0 to 2^k - 1
    slots ← RANDOM(0, (2^k) - 1)
    
    // Calculate wait time in microseconds
    wait_time ← slots * SLOT_TIME
    
    RETURN wait_time
  END FUNCTION
  
  FUNCTION transmit_with_backoff(frame)
    collision_count ← 0
    
    WHILE collision_count < MAX_ATTEMPTS DO
      IF NOT channel_busy() THEN
        TRANSMIT(frame)
        IF collision_detected THEN
          collision_count ← collision_count + 1
          wait_time ← calculate_backoff(collision_count)
          WAIT(wait_time)
        ELSE
          RETURN SUCCESS
        END IF
      ELSE
        // Carrier is busy, wait a random time
        WAIT(RANDOM(1, SLOT_TIME * 100))
      END IF
    END WHILE
    
    RETURN FAILURE  // Too many collisions
  END FUNCTION
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm shows how Ethernet handles collisions: after each collision, the wait time doubles (exponential), reducing the probability of repeated collisions. After 10+ collisions, the maximum wait (1023 slots) is used.
examples.timeComplexity: O(2^n) in worst case where n is number of collisions

examples.title: CSMA/CA Protocol with RTS/CTS
examples.description: Implementation of CSMA/CA with RTS/CTS exchange for wireless networks
examples.explanation: This C code demonstrates CSMA/CA with RTS/CTS handshake: waits for idle channel, performs backoff, sends RTS, waits for CTS, transmits data, then waits for ACK. Uses increasing contention window on failure.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) average case, O(n) worst case with retries
